hypothesis: Incorporating a synthesizability-aware feedback signal derived from a graph neural network trained on experimentally reported synthesis conditions will cause MatAgent to converge on compositionally novel materials with predicted formation energies within target range at a higher rate than formation-energy-only feedback, because penalizing thermodynamically favorable but practically inaccessible compositions will redirect the LLM's iterative reasoning toward chemically accessible substitution pathways, measurably increasing the fraction of proposed materials that are subsequently synthesized and characterized in laboratory validation experiments.
